lead_headline: Restaurant tracker flags 2 new leads today—All Is Well debuts at boutique City Park hotel, Sawa shifts to catering-only—while foreclosures retreat 33% week-over-week to 33 filings.

restaurant.blurb: 2 fresh leads today push the active pipeline to 23 leads (9 openings, 14 closures). New intel: All Is Well restaurant launches at the renovated All Inn Hotel on East Colfax in City Park, part of a $22M boutique conversion. Sawa Mediterranean near University closed its restaurant and pivoted to catering-only after two years. Week's prior leads include G-Que BBQ opening at McGregor Square and Reunion Bread relocating from RiNo to Platt Park.

foreclosure.blurb: 33 new filings this week—down 33% from last week's 49 and snapping a two-week climb. Total exposure: $11.1M, zero filings above $2M. Top case: $628K at 5118 Lumos Lane in Douglas County. 43 auctions scheduled next seven days. Weld County still leads active cases (315), followed by Arapahoe (290) and El Paso (250). Pullback suggests brief reprieve after February's volatility.

reputation.blurb: 7 mentions yesterday brought the seven-day total to 37 citations across 11 sources—the full weekly haul since tracking began (zero prior period). Denver Post dominated with 15 pickups, trailed by Hoodline (5) and social platforms. Friday highlights: Hoodline's Golden Triangle landlord story, Post's Wash Park ice cream closure. Google News RSS captured 36 of 37 discoveries. Steady drumbeat of secondary pickup continues.

subscriptions.blurb: Net +2 yesterday (2 new, 0 canceled) lifted March to +2 MTD (+0.3/day) after midweek churn. The 90-day cumulative now stands at +78 net. Past two weeks show whipsaw pattern: Feb 24's −9 massacre was countered by Feb 23's +5 and Feb 27's +3, but this week wobbled (−1, −4) before Friday's rebound. Longer-term YTD pace of +1.1/day remains above MTD run rate despite volatility.
